2603.21078.title: Assessing the Ability of Neural TTS Systems to Model Consonant-Induced F0 Perturbation
2603.21078.paper_summary: This study proposes a segmental-level prosodic probing framework to evaluate neural TTS models' ability to reproduce consonant-induced f0 perturbation, a fine-grained segmental-prosodic effect that reflects local articulatory mechanisms. We compare synthetic and natural speech realizations for thousands of words, stratified by lexical frequency, using Tacotron 2 and FastSpeech 2 trained on the same speech corpus (LJ Speech). These controlled analyses are then complemented by a large-scale evaluation spanning multiple advanced TTS systems. Results show accurate reproduction for high-frequency words but poor generalization to low-frequency items, suggesting that the examined TTS architectures rely more on lexical-level memorization than on abstract segmental-prosodic encoding. This finding highlights a limitation in such TTS systems' ability to generalize prosodic detail beyond seen data. The proposed probe offers a linguistically informed diagnostic framework that may inform future TTS evaluation methods, and has implications for interpretability and authenticity assessment in synthetic speech.
2603.21078.paper_summary_zh: 這項研究提出了一個段落級的韻律探測框架，以評估神經 TTS 模型重現輔音引起的 f0 擾動的能力，這是一種反映局部發音機制的細緻段落韻律效應。<lb/>我們使用在相同語音語料庫（LJ Speech）上訓練的 Tacotron 2 和 FastSpeech 2，對數千個單詞的合成和自然語音實現進行比較，並根據詞彙頻率進行分層。<lb/>這些控制分析隨後被一項涵蓋多個先進 TTS 系統的大規模評估所補充。<lb/>結果顯示高頻單詞的重現準確，但對低頻項目的泛化能力較差，這表明所檢查的 TTS 架構更依賴於詞彙層級的記憶，而非抽象的段落韻律編碼。<lb/>這一發現突顯了這類 TTS 系統在超越已見數據的韻律細節泛化能力上的限制。<lb/>所提出的探測器提供了一個語言學上知情的診斷框架，可能為未來的 TTS 評估方法提供指導，並對合成語音的可解釋性和真實性評估具有重要意義。
2603.21078.author: Tianle Yang et.al.
2603.21078.authors: Tianle Yang, Chengzhe Sun, Phil Rose, Cassandra L. Jacobs, Siwei Lyu
